review_text: Great service, clean rooms, comfortable beds and great atmosphere.

We verbleven voor de 2e keer op een Tasman park. Deze keer hadden we een economie cottage en deze was van alles voorzien.
De parken zijn echt geweldig.
We hadden wat problemen met de toaster en de vriezer die kapot waren. Ons ijs was hierdoor gesmolten, dus we hadden een melding gemaakt dat de vriezer en de toaster kapot waren. Binnen 15 minuten hadden we een nieuwe vriezer en een nieuwe toaster. We mochten ook nog eens gratis ijs komen pakken bij de receptie. Echt geweldig opgelost en een super service!

El camping está súper mantenido y todas las instalaciones están limpias (cocina, baños), es un lugar tranquilo y el personal es súper amable. Muy recomendado
El clima no dio para disfrutar la piscina, pero se veía que también estaba mantenida
Great place to holiday, it's a little far to walk to the beach or shop but is centrally located between shops and beach.

We stayed here in an economy cabin over the Easter weekend. Firstly I was stoked to find an accommodation option that didn’t charge min 4/5 days for the long weekend and appreciated TPHB allowed us to stay for the long weekend only. ||Our cabin sleeps 4, with a single bunk bed for kids and a queen size bed available. The cabin was very spacious and well kept. Ample cutlery and crockery for cooking with a stove and microwave. The shower was cosy-small but still good nonetheless. The only issue was our bathroom bin hadn’t been emptied from the previous guests.|The little verandah at the doorway was cute and overlooked the pool and kitchen area, only a short 15m walk, making a great cabin option for families where supervision of the kids can be maintained just from the verandah! ||Go for cabins 4-7 for pool access/views and cabins 7-11 for more privacy and quiet away from the pool area. We really liked how the cabins were separate from the van sites. ||Traffic in the park could flow both directions however was a little narrow and could be an issue with vans however we didn’t experience this issue. |There is no playground for kids which was a let down for us with younger kids, as the games area was usually occupied by older kids with attitudes. ||The service we received from booking confirmations to check-in and check out was friendly, down to earth and happy. ||Overall we enjoyed our stay, it was quiet given the Easter holidays and we didn’t hear any road traffic noise during our stay. I’d recommend this park to anyone seeking a quiet park and needing cabin accommodation.
We just stayed here for Easter and had a great stay. Clean amenities, tidy grounds, spacious sites and the staff were very friendly and welcoming. Thanks for having us and keep up the great work 👍

We loved our stay here. The games area and common room were great places for the kids to spread their wings. We enjoyed a bbq night in the shared area. Nice and quiet, family friendly and well looked after. Staff were super friendly too.
Great little park and small dog friendly accomodation as well, clean amenities and very friendly staff.

Our family of 5 had a beautiful stay at Hervey Bay and were pleasantly surprised how quiet and beautiful this park was. Staff were super friendly, and our little fur baby was treated to a little gift on arrival. Thank you to Max and Andrea for welcoming us for our first ever visit in Hervey Bay :)

Still scammers!!!

We stayed here in 2023. We booked the place via booking.com.
We received a payment link from tasman and payed it through that link. But we didn't know they also took our payment through booking.com.
It has been 2.5 years now. And we still didnt reveived our refund for our own money.
Over time we have sended them all the information they wanted from our account to see that they really didn't refund our own money.
Till this day, they didn't refund our money that they received by misleading us by sending us a text message with a link that we had to pay for our stay.

They also stopped replying after getting all the information they have asked for to prove we didn't received any refund.
Our question was and is. Send us the prove that you have refunded us on our creditcard. They are not replying anymore and they still have our money that they received by misleading.

So our question tasman holiday parks.
When will we get our 197 AUD back that we have payed double for our stay?
Bagni puliti, piazzole grandi e ben tenute. Personale gentile e prezzi onesti.
The Tasman holiday park is a beautiful part of the world to stay at.
Camping correct et bien situé. Mériterai un peu de rénovation, en tout cas dans notre cabine.

Central location in Harvey bay with clean amenities…nice quiet park great for families
Great camp kitchen and games room for the kids

Highly recommend this caravan park.  Close to the Esplanade and restaurants.  Has a pool.
The service is awesome and dog friendly too !!
Only here for one night, but excellent facilities. Great location for the esplanade - only 15 minute walk away. Wish we could have stayed there longer. Very clean and quality facilities.

Great little caravan park in Torquay QLD. Amenities were excepts and very clean.
The site had a lot of room.
Slippery floor in shower. Tiny tv is a joke. No coffee table.  Cheap toilet paper.  Very basic cabin.
Friendly service. Calm and beautiful location

The surrounding areas is beautiful,  amazing sunrise and sunsets.
It is so quiet and the owners are amazing people
Lovely Spot
Great price,very accommodated and friendly staff, Clean and tidy have own bathroom and toilet,soap and towels ,kitchen,parking, with pool.highly recommend.
I will holiday there again.

Stayed here a few times... so nice it brings me back. Clean ensuite sites, pool and camp kitchen. Landscaped grounds. AND pet friendly, walk to the beach, roos across the road... and so quiet... what else do you need?

This is a quiet area of Hervey Bay perfect for unwinding. Lots of birds to listen to on the front porch of the beachfront unit we stayed in. Pet friendly, exceptionally clean, self contained unit. Comfy bed, warm shower. I’ll come back again.

This place is amazing 100% recommend staying there if you have pets the cabins are huge plenty of room everything available showers amazing, across road from the water great walking paths nice and quiet, plus the caravan park is very clean blue pool will stay again didn’t want to go home pooches was very happy also stay there you won’t be sorry

And we came back!! What a small, lovely, quiet caravan park, tucked away at Point Vernon. There are permanent residents here, who are friendly and welcoming. Pet friendly, easy walk to the beach, clean ensuites that are huge and accommodating for all bathroom needs. 10 mins from Hervey Bay city and shops. We loved our stay for Christmas with the Grandies. Will be back again! In 2 years. A nice clean up and Managers are lovely! So quiet and relaxing, away from the hassle and bussle.
Pet friendly room which was clean and in a very quiet location. Across the road from lovely walkway near the water.
quiet location with access to great walking tracks along The Esplanade!
Very small and quiet campsite with bathroom on site. We loved it. Kangaroos came along in the morning.

Unique in that all campers have their own roomy ensuite. Sunlodge is small only 4000 sq metres, and very friendly. Camp kitchen is great too. Someone even loaned me a bike yesterday.Beautiful location where you can even spot whales from across the road. Kangaroos lazing and grazing in the quiet streets surrounding the Park ❤️
Third time there, managers always incredibly nice and friendly fellow travellers. En suites are godsend. Quiet area, decent kitchen, pool and short stroll to beach. Recommend it.

We stayed for 4 nights.  Was great for us.  We drove to all of the attractions and then had a peaceful afternoon at the van.
You have your own bathroom which is nice.

Booked an impromptu getaway and booking was very easy even allowed us to come set up earlier than check in time.

The place was absolutely gorgeous, showers and outdoor kitchen have made the plans at home change to make a replica. Went up to book the private shower and toilet and was given it for free and it was beautiful and only an extra $10 a night
The kids absolutely loved walking around seeing all the little spots and they absolutely adored the little fairy house! They also loved the occasional visit from Rosie!
I read some reviews about the sand flies but I just used my usual basic bug away kit and it did the trick but was warned several times about how bad they can be and had the option of getting their good mozzie stuff.

All and all an amazing spot and can’t wait to get back there

We thought this caravan park would be prefect base to leave our van while visiting Fraser Island. The toilet block is pretty cool. However, the midgies are terrible. Worst I have experienced. Also, we were charged a significant extra storage fee without notice when we left our vans plugged into power without any communication while at Fraser.
We left our site two nights early and stayed back in HB.

I’ve hesitated writing this, as I’ve seen the owner replies to any negative reviews of this place. But, after deep reflection and pondering, I can’t not share our extremely disappointing experience. I would strongly caution others before booking.

We booked four nights over Easter, a camp site, the Mango Lodge and “Gertrude the Wagon,” as a special (and very expensive) first camping experience for our friends. We are seasoned caravaners and booked a year in advance. Despite the high fuel costs, and 5-hour drive each way, we chose to go ahead with our booking as we were aware many would be cancelling and we didn’t want to do that to a business. We arrived to… a sandpit. A sandpit infested with sandflies.

Unfortunately our experience was nothing short of a nightmare. Honestly, I would not wish this on anyone. A severe midge infestation & 3 separate repellents later, conditions were made unlivable  - after a long drive, we weren’t able to sleep a wink, while our bodies were crawling with these tiny creatures. As soon as light broke,  we were forced to leave.

What followed was even worse. Instead of any empathy, apology, or attempt to resolve the situation, we were met with a rude and dismissive response and told we would not receive any refund. There has been no follow-up since.

We understand that some circumstances may be outside of a business’s control, but customer service is not. The lack of care, accountability, and basic professionalism here was unacceptable.
A very costly mistake and a deeply disappointing experience. I would not recommend. I’ve taken my case to Fair Trade complete with photo and video evidence. Wish I could give negative stars.

We loved our stay. Nice and quiet. The facilities were great. The kitchen was clean and had everything. The owner was always available and drove us to the check-in for our trip to Fraser Island. All in all a great place, which we would visit again. Greetings from the danish family of 4 🇩🇰🇦🇺

I’ll begin with saying 2 things. 1- the kitchen was impeccable and well stocked, 2- the showers were the best showers possibly EVER but for sure for a campground.

Unfortunately, though, the midges and mosquitos were god awful. No amount of bushman’s or citronella were keeping them away. They got through our (very small) fly screen at night and a week later we’re still up at night itching. If you’ve got a solution for the bugs, highly recommend. Otherwise it’s not your place.

We camped, so there is no real way we can review the "rooms", but my "five star" rating is based on the camping spots. I'm actually writing this review while sitting in front of the camp fire (little awesome fire pits provided)

The service at this place is great. The friendly helpful way the staff here can somehow seamlessly seem like they're not here, (respecting your privacy), and also have everything tended to is phenomenal.

If you're going to stay here, you need to be mindful of the sandflies and the mosquitos. This is clearly written in every piece of information about this place, and if you're local to the area, you will be well aware of the reputation of River Heads, particularly for the sandflies. Good Riddance repellent seems to work the best for them, and, with the aid of smoke from a campfire, you can get around these issues relatively easily.

The location is great! It is beautiful, mostly shady and spacious. There are a great little mob of kangaroos that seem to be semi-frequent and numerous birds. The way it is hidden away off of the road is charming, and the rustic nature of everything here is unique and endearing.

It is pet friendly (with a resident dog that does the rounds to greet everyone), and has horse-riding tracks. Not being a horse owner, I would check with the owners here before booking.

Special mention must be made of the handcrafted facilities and features, like the metalwork sculptures and the showers. So great to see so much time and effort that has gone into these touches. The entry gate is also great, as are the sculptures on the gate.

If you keep the aforementioned insects in mind, and take the precautions, you wouldn't find a better value of money camping area

We stayed at the Mango Cabin — a beautifully designed solid wood cabin that includes a bedroom, a kitchen, and very spacious bathroom and toilet facilities.
There’s access to a garden with a barbecue area, and behind it, an option for an outdoor bath in a kind of jacuzzi.
Up to this point, the place itself is nicely described.
However, the owner of the property doesn’t show much empathy toward her guests. She is very minimalistic and gives the impression that you’re bothering her.

The first time we arrived, there was an issue with entry and timing: after 2:00 p.m., the car is automatically recognized and the gate opens, but before that, you need to note that on the right side of the gate there’s a mailbox with a button. Pressing it sends a call to the owner, who then opens the gate manually.
The owner leaves around 4:00–5:00 p.m., which means that after that hour there’s no one to contact — only an emergency phone number.

If you stay at the cabin, note that garbage is not collected, and there’s no clear information about where to dispose of it. You end up gathering bags of rubbish, but you can’t leave them outside because of the kangaroos in the area.

The kitchen light didn’t work, and on the first evening we were practically in the dark until I figured out that the remote control was out of charge and needed to be recharged.

Although the kitchen is supposed to be fully equipped, in practice there’s no stovetop. If you want to cook, there’s only a small electric pot — so calling it a “well-equipped kitchen” is a bit of an exaggeration.

It should also be mentioned that we stayed over the weekend, when a very loud party with booming music took place nearby — quite the opposite of the peace and quiet promised in the property description.

In conclusion: a place that is unique in its topography, but with the above-noted issues.
A very welcoming feel throughout the entire park. Facilities are spotless and the whole place is beautifully maintained. Will definitely be back

Kangaroos can be seen just around the corner. The showers and toilets are nicely designed and very creative. There are frogs on site, so be careful not to step on them at night. The kitchen is large and well-equipped. The campsites are a bit uneven but have electricity. Water is available at the water-tree near the gate. Be aware of sandflies and mosquitoes on site and bring insect repellent with you!!

Camped here this week, despite being peak season enough space to spread out, not feel jammed in like a Big4.

The amenities were hands down some of the best I’ve ever encountered. The service from staff and up keep of grounds was amazing.

Well done team, already planning the next trip!

We had a great stay here. The facilities are well maintained and we loved the eclectic design. We were on a powered site which facilitates 4 sites around a central pole with plenty of room to spread out, as the owners offer cable and hoses to spread out... and there is plenty of space to spread. Unfortunately not everyone took advantage of this which was disapponting but not the sites responsibility. Fire pits supplied and we utilised the doggy day care to visit K'Gari. Great location and very enjoyable stay!

Spoke to Suzanne over the phone today to cancel my booking - which I was cancelling due to the complete lack of respect she had when talking to me and my partner on previous occasions while trying to organise our stay. At no point during any of our communications was I rude or hostile, in fact I was calm & polite on all occasions. I can only imagine this would be so much worse should I actually have an issue with my booking if we decided to stay there in the end. She consistently ignored my emails when all I was asking about was more information on a tour or how the transfer works. I asked her why she was so hostile with me and she responded that i was asking questions about the tour when its on the website. She was also pissed off that my partner rang to ask the same questions. That doesn't exactly make sense to me. Dont offer the service if you cant fulfill it! She spoke horribly to me and hung up on me while i was in the middle of talking. She kept saying all the information was on the website. If it was on the website I wouldnt need to call. Based on other low star reviews, it appears that this isnt the first time this sort of thing has happened. If you are so allergic to customer service then change your profession ! :)
Lovely quite spot, fantastic utilities and great hosts - no camping on top of each other and it's gated!

We absolutely loved our stay at Fraser Coast RV. The location is great as close to amenities but with lots of space to camp in peace. The facilities were so clean and well thought out. The owners were so helpful and we took them up on the doggy day care while we did a Fraser Island day trip. They were so lovely and reassuring. Highly recommend staying here and we will definitely go back.

Loved our stay at Fraser Coast RV park. We did 3 nights and hopped over to Fraser Island for 1 leaving our van behind.
The owners were friendly and helpful. The facilities were great and super clean. Had the best showers. Lots of space for the kids to run. State of origin was on the big screen.
We loved staying here with a small family. And great spot to visit Fraser Island. And no midges! We stayed at the start of July.
Would stay again.

Loved Fraser Coast RV park. |We had a great stay for 3 nights. Hopped over to Fraser Island for 1 and left our van behind. They have the best showers, super clean facilities and lots of space for the kids. |You can have a fire and as state of origin was on they had it up in the stage area on a big screen. |A really great friendly park with all the amenities and space. Thank you for a great stay.

We stayed at Mango Lodge, and WOW, we were so impressed. The beds were so comfortable, the lodge was well equipped, and the outside bath was amazing.

The RV park was excellent, seeing the kangaroos graising in the mornings, plenty of room for the girls to run around, the communal amenities were clean and well equipped and the camp fire at night was a great place to meet other campers.

Paul and Suzanne and Rosie, the dog, couldn't do enough for us and made sure our stay was memorable.

We had an excellent stay at Tasman Holiday Park in Hervey Bay. The value for money was outstanding, and everything was exceptionally clean. The camp kitchen was spotless and fully equipped with everything you might need—plenty of appliances, plus thoughtful extras like dish soap, sponges, and kitchen towels.

We were genuinely impressed. The park was quiet and peaceful, never overcrowded, which made for a very relaxing atmosphere. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a clean, well-maintained, and tranquil camping experience.

For hervey bay this is good for longer stays.
It's not as expensive as most.
The toilet blocks are very clean. Good kitchen and it has a pool.

There are a couple more expensive caravan parks which are more resort like. But this is good value
Very noisy area.

We stayed on ensuite,powered site.
Quiet Park,not too far from major attractions. Friendly staff,clean and well maintained
This was the least value for money park during our 6 week trip. Expensive, small site, quite a distance to the beach, lots of permanents.

Dog friendly. Cabins are clean and spacious- the bathroom is a lot more outdated than the cabin but that was not a bother to us. It was raining so we did not access the pool area. Well equipped big outdoor kitchen area with a lot of seating and Smart TV. It has an air fryer, pans, pots, cutlery and utensils- 2 big industrial fridges if needed also. Staff was lovely we did late check in however my in laws got a dog goodie bag upon check in which was lovely. Easy check in and check out process. Would recommend.

We based ourselves here for a few days with our van while we explored the surrounding areas. |It’s nice and central and we enjoyed being minutes to the Esplanade and handy to shops etc. |While we were travelling down the coast this was our most expensive stay at $79 per night for a powered site. |The sites are small and access is pretty tight. By the time we were in and set up we had about 1.5 metres to a tent next door and about 1 metre to a camper trailer on the other side. |The amenities were ok and kept clean throughout our stay and there’s also a nice looking pool for swimming. However in the laundry two of the washing machines were ‘out of order’ with signs on them which was inconvenient for a number of us who were trying to catch up on some washing. |The road noise is pretty full on too and runs through the night.
Tidy rooms, good location
Using caravan for my accommodation, facilities are clean and the park is well serviced. Close to shopping, clubs and beach.
Nice place central to everything

One of the cleanest campsite bathrooms I’ve ever seen!
The facilities were spotless and very well maintained throughout our stay. You can really tell that cleanliness is a top priority here. Everything was regularly cleaned, fully stocked, and in excellent condition. It made our camping experience so much more comfortable. Highly recommended!
Ensuite site was clean and tidy.   Disappointing but was it backed onto  a main road which was very noisy.  Park itself is well maintained amd service friendly.  A shame about the road noise.

We booked a three day tour to K’Gari and we stayed the day before and the day after the tour in the Tasman caravan park. This was one of the nicest and cleanest caravan  parks we’ve been to on our road trip on the entire East Coast. We loved it here!

Absolutely Love this little gem. I was staying weekly as I set up my new business. I now stay monthly & it's my home away from home.  Staff are super friendly & accommodating. Rooms are always clean. They Have everything i need.
Really nice park well maintained

We booked a Superior 2 bedroom cabin for NYE with our family. We tried to call before arrival to see if we could have an earlier check in. No answer we tried about 10 times. We arrived at 1.30pm to check in. Upon arrival we were told that our cabin won't be ready until after 2pm as they are busy and running behind. They said they'd call us when ready. It got to 3.15 and no phone call, we have 3 kids and had just driven 3 hours. We were also so told that the pool facilities weren't working as someones child did a code brown the day before, she laughed as she told me this ... not really funny!!!
so you can imagine it's NYE in 30 degree heat, no check in yet and no pool facilities! We were told we could drive 15min away to use another facilities pool.. not ideal when you don't want to drive anymore during your stay! Finally at 3.30pm when we drove back to check in the cleaner was still in the room and told me the cabin was filthy from previous holiday maker and she was still behind. Not ideal. We finally got to check into our cabin (no 16).. well what a let down. It's nothing like the photos, the cabin is old run down, dirty, tiles.chipped cracked, stains on floor and carpet. It was nothing like the photos and to be honest false advertising. I went back and spoke to reception and they couldn't care less, they wanted out for the afternoon and told me the photos are just like that for advertising, totally false! So be warned and I'll attach photos the Superior cabins are just like the standard ones. The bunk beds are very dangerous for young children to get on and off the ladders. Don't waste your money, better value for money elsewhere! The park itself lacks any real holiday atmosphere and staff couldn't care less!

Location, and amenities were clean and tidy. However the  gate opening at 2pm only gives you afew hours of light before it’s dark after you set up. So in at 2pm and out at 10am is only 8hrs and not enough time to explore the surroundings.
I found the owners to be unfriendly and not flexible. We won’t be returning!!!

A wonderful camp ground, clean and clever facilities. Manager was friendly but not 'in your face', and a great place to leave our van while we explored K'Gari (Fraser). Easy access, clear communication.

We booked to stay at Fraser Coast RV Park for three nights.

The amenities were so cool, I loved the rustic vibe. The camp areas are smaller than expected.

Location is great if you are wanting to go to Fraser or Hervey Bay.

Unfortunately we got absolutely annihilated by midges during our visit and had to leave early due to our toddler also being very uncomfortable. We purchased all different types of repellent, coils, even the repellent the owners recommended, dressed in long sleeve, however we were unlucky. We did however get a refund from good riddance and they provided us with some lotion to assist with our bites.

Overall a good stay but really be prepared for the midges, unfortunately mine are still extremely itchy after 3 weeks.
1 STAR REVIEW REMAINS DUE TO THE VITRIOLOC RESPONSE FORM THE OWNER

Der Platz ist sehr schön und die sanitären Einrichtungen ebenfalls.
Leider ist es unmöglich das zu genießen, da es überall kleine Stechmücken hat. Diese kommen sogar bei geschlossenen Türen durch die kleinsten Lücken (Klimaanlage oder Lüftung) in den  Camper. Wir wurden regelrecht gefressen. Es ist unaushaltbar und wir haben seit einer Woche hunderte Stiche auf unserer Haut.
Da können die Besitzer des Platzes nichts dafür, aber es ist trotzdem schrecklich gewesen.

Schade, da der Platz ohne Mücken super und 5 Sterne wäre….
Absolutely lovely place, must go! So vintage and have everything you need. Would totally recommend. Bring mosquito spray tho!

We stayed at Fraser Coast RV Park for two nights and really appreciated how well-equipped it was. The amenities were great — clean showers, a kitchen with an oven, microwave, and even a coffee machine. It had everything we needed for a comfortable stay.

The location is also very convenient, especially if you’re planning to catch the ferry to K’gari (Fraser Island).

Unfortunately, there were a lot of midges during our visit. We even used the product recommended by the park, but still ended up covered in bites. It got so itchy we decided to leave after the first night.

Overall, it’s a lovely spot with great facilities, but definitely be prepared for the bugs — especially if you’re sensitive to bites.

Very chill and nice owner (honorouble mention to rosy the campmanager).
Very cute accomodation, everything diy zelf made
Great showers, very well equipped bush kitchen and lots of space! Very friendly and welcoming.
I will never go back in this camping. I had a lot of mosquito’s.

What an amazing unique and special place this is, it has to be seen to be believed.
Great stay, the best showers we have ever had!
Lovely owners and stunning wildlife.
For those complaining about bugs or internet, it’s common sense to have some spray, and just use your 4G. Kids can go exploring and use their imaginations, it’s the perfect escape.

What an amazing place with greatest hosts ever! We had a great time at the campground that is designed so beautiful and inventive! Lovely
Btw… At the entry you will see plenty of kangaroos

Caravans need to be aware there is no dump point. The closest is Hervey Bay. 20min Drive.
If you are here in summer the mosquitos and midges are probably some of the worst you’ll get anywhere in Australia. They sell Good Riddance at the office but we found it was useless. You may need something stronger.
Don’t forget you can’t get in before 2:00pm

Worst of my life. Woke to the van infested in midges. Sheets, clothes and ourselves. We had woke up at 3am scratching and realised the state of the place when we got up. There was literally no escape. Had to leave the place asap and spent the following morning and another 40 dollars in the laundromat washing everything we had in the van and disinfecting the van. Days later I am still covered in marks. On contacting the camp the were very dismissive of my concern or issues too which was disappointing considering everything. Road down to it is completely broken up so you would have to be very careful. We arrived at night and had to drive on the grass from the road into the gate. Would avoid at all costs and will never go again needless to say

A big thank you to Suzanne and Paul for making our stay so enjoyable! From the moment we arrived, they were incredibly welcoming and went above and beyond to ensure we had a great time. They took such amazing care of our dog, Byron, while we explored Fraser Island. Receiving daily photos of Byron enjoying walks and relaxing on the couch with them made us feel completely at ease. It was clear she felt right at home, and we couldn’t have asked for better care.

The park itself is exceptional – peaceful, quiet, and beautifully maintained. The camp kitchen was fully equipped with everything we needed, even offering some fun games for rainy days. Suzanne and Paul also provided fantastic tips on ferries, places to visit, and things to do, which made our trip even better.

Everything was easy, and nothing was too much trouble for them. The park is in a great location, right near the gateway to Fraser Island, and it had everything we needed. We thank Suzanne and Paul so much for their hospitality and care, and we can’t wait to come back again this year!

Cheers,

Jaiden, Bruna and Byron

편견 1도 없는 여주인은 두차량의 아시안 가족과 프랑스인 가족을 단지 같은 회사의 같은 사양의 쌍둥이 같은 캠퍼밴을 타고 왔다는 이유로, 단지 동시에 입장했다는 이유로, 단지 먼저 진입했다는 이유로, 심지어 단지 동시에 차에 내려 차량 인원을 간발의 시간차로 말했다는 이유로, 1박 요금을 우리에게 몰아서 받으려 함. 각자 다른 가족에게 받으려 시도하지 않았을까? 지금도 의문임. `아니라고 저 가족과는 모르는 사이라고` 했음에도 굳이 안에 있는 남주인을 불러서 전화예약 여부를 확인한 이유는 지금도 의문임. 나중에 이메일로 보낸 영수증에 admin fee가 붙어 물어보니 보안문제로 사전예약 후 입장허가 관련비용이라며. 20여 일 머무며 입장료 받은 유일한 밴파크.

이것 외에는 깨끗한 주방, 특별한 컨셉의 화장실, 조용한 파크, 그리고 불멍.

Sehr nett und mit viel Liebe zum Detail! Gerne mal vorbeischauen!

Besonders Marc war sehr sehr nett und hat uns sogar noch Garnelen gebracht, die wir dann an der sehr schönen Outdoorküche gebraten haben :)

Wenn in River Heads, dann dort!

First impressions were great until we got to the camp site. 3 groups of us booked and paid for 5 nights however left after the second night, should have left after the first but it’s a bit of a drive for 1 night. Sand flys galore! We bought every repellant you could. Candles, spray, cream, coils and nothing works. The only reason I gave 2 stars was the ammenities were nice though needed the repellant in hand for that too.
Would definitely not return.
Great campsite, lovely trees and great birds

Great location, fantastic facilities. Only downside are the resident midges which were very partial to biting us. Would highly recommend using Good Riddance insect repellant as this was the only one that worked consistently for us.
Hosts were informative and helpful. The grounds were well kept.
Caravan site with ensuite clean nice hot showers
Great spot . Beautiful views , great walking paths , dog friendly , very clean , good friendly service , peaceful , awesome parks , magic spot
Best doggy cabin I've ever stayed in

Great spot for caravan, pet friendly. Not too busy even on Easter weekend. Few steps away from the pet friendly beach, which is a great spot for sunset watching. The private toilet for each spot is a nice bonus. There's a swimming pool and shared bbq/fridge/toaster kettle/ microvawe as well on site
Very tidy and quiet lovely spot

Stayed with our caravan friendly Staff.
Nice & quiet tourist park.
Cabins also available.

We spent three nights over the new year and really appreciated the peaceful, friendly vibes at Sunlodge. The amenities were top notch with a clean and spacious ensuite at each site. The clean outdoor camp kitchen was well appointed. And we made the most of the refreshing and well-maintained pool on a couple of those steamy Queensland days. The gardens are manicured, and the laundry was a welcome bonus after a week on the road. Thanks to Tanya and Darren, the loveliest hosts (even when I locked our key in the ensuite!!!). Will highly recommend Sunlodge to everyone.
Absolutely LOVE the view. Nice quiet area, beautiful for walks

Tiny campground, dated but very clean. Felt very safe, lovely staff.|Good facilities. Right by the Esplanade drive. Close to everything.|Nice people onsite,  roos close by!. Ensuites a bonus. Washing facilities great. Staff very friendly|
The best dog friendly cabin we have ever stayed at,right across from beach to walk the dogs, hosts are absolutely the best people, 5 stars 🤩

You really know you have enjoyed a place when you get to the end of your stay and the question is asked “Do we really have to leave?”.
This lovely little park is old style with each site having its own amenities block. These are dated but very clean. Our site (5) was next to the camp kitchen but this was no issue at all. It it looked well equipped and was kept very clean.
On arrival we discovered we were also parked next to the pool pump which was operating at the time but a quick call to the owner assured us that it would turn off at 4pm, which it did.
Our hosts Darren and Vicky were very friendly and helpful with local knowledge suggestions when asked.
We were booked for 4 nights but wished it was longer. Would definitely stay again.

What a fabulous hidden gem!! I took 5 children to Sunlodge last weekend and we ALL thoroughly enjoyed the experience! Firstly, the owners were sooo friendly, helpful and welcoming from booking to offering to help everyone with their camp set-ups. The sites had individual ensuites, a real luxury! The pool was beautifully clean and the kitchen too which had the best bbq. The location was fantastic in that we were a short walk to either parts of the beautiful Point Vernon esplanade and rock pools. The kids absolutely loved participating in the Lorikeet feeding done daily too so highly recommend this lovely little park. Thankyou so much Darren and Tania for a great weekend!🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟

In short, the location is excellent, public boat ramp is near-by, and this is pretty much all that is good about it.

Used to stay there for 2 nights several years ago while on long weekend vacation. Back then, the experience was "well above average". Now the new owners apparently do not care about the customer service: could not even take the booking correctly!

Communal kitchen is dirty, has roach infestation, no hot water in the tap. Seems to be treated as a "zero maintenance property". The sea-water pool is filthy (admittedly, did not risk it, just gave a glance).

Positively not recommended for an overnight/two nights stay, especially with a tent or a camper van. If living in a larger camper for a week or two, may be Ok-ish if your standards are on the low side of the spectrum. The cabins are not recommended as the site is too crowded and poorly maintained.

Not coming back.

Great little caravan park. Small park with each site having its own ensuite. Ensuites are clean and tidy as with the rest of the grounds. Very quite part of the world, not far from everything you need in Hervey Bay. Not far to the water  either. The office isn't open every day. However, when you arrive, there is an envelope at the office with your ensuite key and site map inside. We had site 7, which was easy to reverse our larger van. Would definitely recommend it if you are looking for a quiet spot.

We had a wonderful visit, the 1st of more.
Onsuites to powered sites, pet friendy, tranquil with direct access to ocean front walks will ensure we return.

Good for a backup campground
Has some interesting features but not really worth the price there's basically nothing to do
Very boring

Our 1st visit, certainly not our last. Such a beautiful part of the world. A wonderful surprise to realize not your regular caravan park, more like a farm stay with heaps of room whether a group or single site. It is so welcoming you feel like part of their family on a drop in holiday! The attention to detail makes it feel so homely, and is also so clean. I urge everyone to go stay for such a unique & colourful experience! Well done Suzanne & Paul. You have clearly worked extremely hard to produce something so beautiful. We will be back!

For 39$ you have so much space and calm in a big park!
At just 20min from Hervey bay, it is a great and best value for money location!
The owner is really nice! And amnesties really great
Bush setting, not cheek to jowl bin fires ok,pets ok. Limited power and water, toilets showers a bit rustic but adequate. Liked it a lot!

Best Rv park we have ever stayed at
Spacious sites kept immaculate including amenities
By owners Suzanne Paul and Rosie the dog.
Innovating camp kitchen showers toilets everything is fantastic
My partner broke her arm and we needed to extend our stay ,again nothing was to much of a problem helping us out .
Camp fire every night
Highly recommend to all
Thankyou so so much
Cheers Shane and Christine

We will be back! Amazing property with acres to explore with the kids. Suzanne, Paul and Rosie 🐶 are fantastic hosts, the RV Park was in great form for the annual River Heads Rock weekend. Fun times had by all. We extend our stay every year. See you next time!

I travelled a lot across Australia, stayed at many camping and caravan parks. I gave to this one two stars because of:

1. the lady owner was rude and unfriendly
2. it was a lot of dust on the site
3. wi-fi worked only near internet caffe
4. it was quite cold in the showers and toilets

I don't recommend this place.

What an amazing place, a piece of paradise. Quiet during the day, then a bit busy during meal times as everyone comes together to use the unique camp kitchen. It has everything you need to cook a lovely meal. Great view of nature while you eat too. I stayed in Gertrude the Gypsy Wagon. It was amazing. Comfy bed, air cond or a little heater to use as well if needed. Touch lights, infuser so its smells delightful when you enter. Use of your own outback dunny, which is very clean. Outdoor area has a table with 2 chairs. Lovely spot yo sit and watch the wildlife. Definitely recommend this beautiful place. Owners are fantastic too, very helpful. Great place to spend a few days to get back to peace and quiet.

Such a wonderful place 💜 peaceful, unique and beautifully creative! They looked after our girl, Willow the American Akita, whilst we visited K’gari. The best doggy day care ever! Thank you for looking after our girl with such care 💜
gteat place all good

Just had a glorious week on a powered site in our caravan. Amenities were immaculate, fire pits supplied and were awesome for this time of year. Paul and Suzanne Taylor were so welcoming informative and friendly hosts.
The park is budget friendly in one of the most beautiful places in our country. I would highly recommend to all ages and couples/families.
Fantastic place to camp, great environment away from the crowd s. Probably just a little bit expensive.

The staff is very kind and helpful! Regarding the kitchen, this has been the best campsite I’ve ever been so far, it was very well equipped and quite clean. Also, decoration and lighting is great. Showers and toilets are also good. The only bad part about this camping is the big amount of mosquitoes, there are TOO MANY, but of course this is normal if you are in nature. However, it is crazy how many there are, you cannot stay outside, you need to be in your van or tent. I would repeat with more bite cream and repellent.

Such a well laid out park! Space galore! Amenities are clean and well functioning- water pressure is great!

Bring mozzie coils, candles, sprays etc... the midgies and mozzies are a bit brutal due to all the rain.

Passage rapide d’une courte nuit avant un départ pour Fraser Island. Nous avons campé avec notre voiture et notre rooftent. Les commodités sont propres, une cuisine extérieure à disposition, décoration, accueil, sécurisant.  Le seul - : les moustiques +++.
Nous avons donc vu que de nuit. Dommage qu’on ne puisse rester plus longtemps à Hervey bay pour y séjourner une nuit de +.

En rigtig skøn og rolig plads, med alle faciliteter, både det simple og det luksuriøse, kan varmt anbefales til en tur til K'gari, ejeren sørger for booking og transport til opsamlingsbus til færgen som kun er få km. fra pladsen 🌞⛴️,og så er man omgivet af vild natur og kænguruer, det gør jo ikke pladsen ringere 🦘🐬

Stayed here for 1 night over the long weekend. Had originally booked lovers lane however on arrival the owners let us know that had swapped sites for us to be closer to amenities which was nice. We had dog corner all to ourselves which had plenty of room for us to spread out. Flushing toilets & beautiful showers! Loved the showers so much I wanted to take one home haha, I swear it was one of the best showers I’ve ever had I didn’t want to get out! The bush kitchen was super handy too. Lovely owners with lots of handy information about the place & surrounds. We also made a mistake with our booking date and they were happy to fix for us with no dramas! We will definitely be back for a longer stay next time.
Midges & mozzies were a bit annoying but they gave fair warning on their website about it and also gave us the option of purchasing some repellent from them :) ours clearly wasn’t up to scratch but that was our fault :)
Also pet friendly :)

HELL on Earth. Booked for three nights, left in the first 3 seconds. Front gate doesn’t even open unless someone opens it for you, meaning no owner no access. There is only one person there in the entire park…the owner, who charges an arm and a leg for a 100mL bottle of insect repellent. Middle of nowhere and extremely sketchy. No beaches, pool, or life except for the mosquitoes eating you alive. Don't stay there in summer, winter, spring, autumn. Be thankful you saw this review and run! No refund either good riddance!

Four of us stayed here before going to Fraser for a week, and it was amazing! The owners were extremely friendly and chatted to us about the history of the buildings and the campsite. Awesome little area of nature, definitely coming back!

You could not have lovelier host/managers  , so hospitable could not help us enough, the gardens are extremely well maintained , it’s got a wonderful pool to take a dip in and the camp kitchen makes you feel your in an island paradise |It is a. Small park so as you can imagine it books quickly but every site comes with ensuite which are huge the biggest we have seen on our travels |We really could not have found a better place to stay |We were so pleased and loved our stay

Very accomodating and comfy stay with my family and dog. Staff were so helpful, they even helped change our flat tyre! The room was very clean and beds were perfect! A short drive to bars and restaurants.

Great place to stay, great views from the cabin's, crystal clear swimming pool, great camp kitchen,
Rob and Vicki, great people to deal with

Loved my stay here! The Managers and staff are welcoming, very friendly and most helpful!  Clean facilities, a swimming pool and cabana with a well equipped kitchenette available to use. Your own ensuite toilet and shower is included with your powered site! Very good value for money. Pets are welcome as well!
Lovely small, older style caravan park. Really quiet and easy walk to Pt Vernon beaches and reserve walking tracks. Hosts are kind and very helpful. Looking forward to our return visit.

Just had a great stay at Sunlodge in Point Vernon, close enough to everything but still away from the busyness of the Bay.  Really comfortable park on the beachfront; rooms were extremely clean and everything was available; and best of all we were able to take our furbabies !!  Will definitely be back !!,   Thank you!

Awesome location all sites have ensuites, small park.
Staff are great.

What a lovely small caravan park, located across the road from the beach but away from the hustle and bustle of Hervey Bay. Tight for caravan reversing but Darren is a master at guiding you in. We also had a cabin, which was clean and nicely laid out, with ocean views. Tanya and Darren, the owners, we're friendly and helpful. Would definitely stay at this park again.

Only planned on staying for a month and ended being at this wonderful caravan park for 3 months. Such a lovely park, clean & tidy.  Can’t thank Rob & Vicki enough, they made our stay so easy. Nothing was ever too much trouble for this amazing couple. Sadly we are moving on, but we will definitely be back. Julie & Chris

Firstly my husband & I camped here for a week & we love the bush style camping & the amenities are awesome,love the rustic look😊
We got a nice beautiful shady powered spot which we were extremely happy with.
However we had issues with the electric gate not opening to come back in after leaving the park!
At one point we left & headed to the local shops & when we got back we tried the gate number & the office number for about a half hour with no success! In the end I had to climb through the barbed wire fence to see if I could find someone to help us get back in.
In the end another camper opened it from the inside.This was an inconvenience to say the least!
As a business owner, you wouldn't leave shop when something as important as an entrance & exit gate isn't working sufficiently.
Other than that & the annoying midges we enjoyed our stay 😁😁

We loved our stay here. Even though arriving late we advised the owners and they still greeted us on arrival, providing us with maps of the sites and letting us stay at a spot closer to the amenities than what we had booked as it was available.
Lots of old australiana charm, toilets and showers were clean and camp kitchen was very useful.
In retrospect we should have taken the owners offer to purchase their beg repellent as the sprays we had had little effect. We hadn't been bitten so bad anywhere else we've camped.

It was great nature surrounding camp site with a lot of space for my boys to sent up a tent next to van. The showers was impressive with the big brass ring as shower head and the hot water was so great to have a good shower. Owners were very helpful with extension power cords etc. The only disappointing thing was the mozzies and midges/sand flies- inspire of an early wood fire and lots of insect repellent spray, they still managed to sting us terribly.

Super Campingplatz im gemütlichem Bush-Ambiente. Sehr Sauberes WC, Dusche und Campingküche!
Sehr sehr netter Camping Chef, gibt super Tipps & Tricks für den Aufenhalt im Bush und dem Umgang mit den Moskitos :-)

Sind von dort aus mit dem Camper zur Fähre für nach K‘gari, ist perfekt, nur 3-4 Minuten mit dem Auto.
Beautiful
One of the nicest RV-Parks we've experienced. Very friendly folks and a beautifully maintained camp ground located right in the middle of  gorgeous nature. Pete, Tim and Simone from Germany

Campingplatz mitten in der Natur

Dieser tolle Campingplatz liegt mitten in der Natur, umringt von Wald. Die Atmosphäre ist sehr entspannt, da viel Platz vorhanden ist. Die sanitären Einrichtungen sind mit Liebe gebaut und sehr sauber. Die Eigentümer geben sich sehr viel Mühe, dass man sich hier wohlfühlt. Wir haben unseren Aufenthalt vor Ort sogar noch einmal verlängert.
Not worth the effort

Australiana themed RV Park with novel amenities block, camp kitchen, wash house and disabled facilities featuring rusted corrugated iron.  Plenty of room to spread out in bush setting.  Secure entry and exit, your phone number will be verified.  If by chance the gate is open on arrival, drive direct to office where this will be done.  Lots of walking trails.  Loved this park.

Absolutely loved our stay at this immaculately maintained park. So much love, time and effort put in by the owners to create and maintain this fabulous park. Owners were very accommodating when we had vehicle issues and needed to extend our stay. Lots of room to spread out and set up, unlike so many parks today. We also loved our daily visits and pats with Rosie, the four-legged park manager.  We can't wait to come back and stay again.
Mango Lodge is beautiful. Highly recommended for a comfy stay. Come in July when River Heads Rocks is on for amazing entertainment too.

Perfect camping spot! Honestly this is 5 star camping. The owners are sharing their little slice of paradise. There is a lot of space, facilities are great and beautiful as well ! People are very friendly! I would always stay here when I'm in the area. Perfect for 1 night or a longer stay as well
Beautiful campsite in the bush with clean facilities. Stayed 2 nights in the middle of wildlife and enjoyed it very much

Poor value, overpriced tiny cabin with basic fitout, beds that are not made up, non soundproof walls, and only one aircon for the entire cabin. For context, we paid $362 per night over the peak holiday season. It's probably worth $180 in reality - basically think basic cabin at caravan park, nothing more.

I wanted to stress that the two-bedroom cabin we stayed in was really really small, and the photos on the website and Booking.com looked a lot bigger, and considering the price we paid, I thought it was also meant to be bigger after seeing the photos online.

The only saving grace was really friendly service at check in. Although I'm not sure if I'm happy to pay $362 a night for a few minutes of encounter with a lovely staff. I am really dreading my second night stay tomorrow and wished I can cancel.

WATCH OUT!
(THEIR REACTION HERE ON GOOGLE REVIEW IS NOT TRUE. THEY DONT RESPOND VIA EMAIL AND ARE LYING IN THEIR REACTION. They didn't send the money back.)

1 time via booking.com and the 2nd they charged my creditcard themselves.
It has been 10 months, almost 11, that I am trying to get my money back. Booking tried to help. And they promised booking to send the double charged money back. They did not. Despite all the information they have asked me and I have send this to them. They are not sending my money back.

@Tasman Holiday Parks. When are you transferring my double charged money back to my account?

Beautiful park great location
Has nice cabins good size
Very clean
Pool and BBQ area
Great spot and good price

Grundsätzlich waren wir sehr zufrieden, mit unserer Cabin. Der Parkplatz war direkt vor dem Häuschen, es war sauber und die kleine Kochecke hatte mit Herdplatte, Toaster und Wassekocher alles, was zwei Personen benötigen. Der Essbereich und das Badezimmer waren klein, was aber nicht wirklich störte. Der WLAN-Empfang war jedoch in dem ausserhalb des Parks gelegenen Häuschen leider nicht gegeben.

To be clear, this review is of the Scarness Tasman Park.
I took an ensuite site, which unfortunately all are next to an extremely loud road. The ensuite itself was clean, except the roof is mouldy and the sink was blocked (which I fixed).
The park itself is nice and quiet, but the road noise is terrible. Maybe the people who said it is quiet here were deaf or had a site up the back? Maybe they had the wrong caravan park?

Myself and my children had 3 days here. It was very comfortable and clean. The units were modern and well equipped with everything you could want for a family holiday. It was a fantastic location as the beautiful esplanade was a short 5 minutes walk away.

Super clean ablutions, lovely well maintained sites, quiet and the staff really friendly and helpful. A lot of permanents but no problem at all. Good laundry, lots of washing lines, in close proximity to the shops and about a km to the esplanade/foreshore. Enjoyed our two week stay and felt sad to leave.
Above review pretty old. The new review now in Aug 2024 I complained about the barking dogs and the fact that there are no rules about the dogs in the park. I don't see this review posted. I had no major complaints about anything else

Third time staying at the Tasman, twice in a caravan and this time in a cabin. Everything was clean, the resort itself nicely maintained. The staff very friendly and helpful. The kitchen could have been better equipped... a couple of bowls for fruit salad and a green salad for instance. The old frying pan was replaced which was nice. A few coat hangers would have been useful. A bit far to walk to the beach and probably best to use the car for the supermarket. However, our time spent at the park was completely  spoiled by the dog in the caravan next to us who barked non stop at everyone that walked by. Still barking at 9pm. We spoke to the owner of the dog on arrival who said " nothing I can do if he barks at people walking by". This is not good enough as we all pay to have a peaceful holiday. In future we will look for caravan parks that don't have dogs or at least some rules about keeping dogs quiet.
Very good.
Park is super stil en zeer netjes. Sommige plekken zijn wel gelegen aan de doorgaande weg. Overdag hoor je dit wel maar ‘s nachts nietzo.

Average at best. We stayed in a cabin, the bed was terrible, too soft and sunk into the middle. Unable to block out all the outside lights at night when trying to sleep, alot of light came through the blinds. Bathroom old and required repairs. We won't be back.
Great place to stay , very quiet, staff friendly and helpful, amenities very clean .
Comfy bed, lovely gardens, good pool.

Deluxe Cabin was Filthy, switch hanging out of power point. Filthy couch stained and old food under cushions and sticky when sat on, holes in flooring, black mould in Bath room. TV didn't work and zero wifi. Huge stretch calling this Deluxe more like in need of Major Maintenance and Cleaning.

Clean park good beds … but the cabin 8 my sister and I shared was lacking in towel rails, was too high and wasn't big enough for 2 towels let alone for 4 people (which the cabin suppose to accommodate) it needs a shelf  to put our toiletries/make up on and a couple of hooks to hang clothes on while showering would be helpful. In the shower there was one small rack should be a bigger one. These are all basics for a cabin. We didn’t even shower in the cabin we both used the shower block facility as it had the hooks and a shelf which is what should be in the cabin!
Beautiful place to stay, clean, quiet, and wonderful amenities.  Super friendly staff and our little dog loved it too. Definitely coming back to stay again.  Thank you for your wonderful hospitality.
The rooms are very good but there is still improvements to be made which they are on to.  95% renovated so when completed they will be amazing.

I stayed in a deluxe cabin, it was very clean and updated. Check in took 2 minutes, and Bec was friendly and easy going. I had to extend my stay at extra night, Bec rearranged things to accommodate me.
It's about 10 minutes walk to Stocklands shopping centre
I would gladly stay here again.
Perfect!

We are travelling with our small dog and were really appreciative of this pet friendly park. The cabin we stayed in was lovely and cool after a long day of travelling. The only down side was a distinct dampness odour when entering the cabin which could be rectified with some essential oil. It was well equipped with most things we needed and the bed was comfortable. We enjoyed some relaxation time in the pool and it’s a nice walk to the beach front & shops.|We originally only booked one night but were offered a second night at a good rate so we took the offer.

Enjoyed our stay, clean rooms
However whenever we go away we always take our own pillows & pillowcases
So when we arrived placed our pillows on bed then supplied pillows in the wardrobe
We left before office opened only to get a call from the manager asking where the pillows where   Would have thought he would have checked before making a phone call to us on our way home
Just left as as assumed thieves!
Liked everything else about our stay except phone call from manager no apologies
When it was his mistake not checking wardrobe

Recently stayed here in a cabin and were very impressed. It has the best camp kitchen I've ever come across and literally everything you need from crockery and cutlery, fresh dish clothes, tea towels and every kitchen utensil possible. You could literally just bring food and not be disappointed. Kids enjoyed the pool and the laundry even had free powder! Some cabins even allow small dogs inside. Cabin was clean and never ran out of hot water.

This place is perfect, it's awesome for children, adult get aways, and pets 😊. Close to the beach and on the main road. 100% suggest this place for a weekend get away or a good holiday. Lovely hosts who will do anything to ensure your stay and service is beautiful 😍.
카라반 사이트에 텐트치고 5일 있었읍니다. 사이트당 촤대 6인 기준이었는데 카라반 이용할 경우에나 6명 가능할 것 같고 텐트로는 좁아서 힘들어요. 직원분들 모두 하나같이 친절하셔서 너무 좋았고 시설도 깨끗하게 잘 되어있었읍니다. 다만 개미가,, 부쉬캠핑 할 때보다 많았어요,,, 메인도로 바로 옆이라 밤새 차소리로 시끄러우니 참고하새오.

Booked in and paid 4 a wk in advance 4 a powered site only to get a phone call and hr b4 we arrived saying that they had a double booking and we're out of luck find somewhere else which I have never had happen anywhere else in my travels around Australia

We had a caravan.  Accommodated as requested next to other family with a camper trailer in the camping section.  The grounds were extremely clean, neat and tidy.  The bathrooms and toilets were always clean and well maintained.  Camp kitchen well stocked.  Large camp fridge available and a TV.  Saturday afternoon sausage sizzle in camp kitchen. Pool clean and tidy.  Everything was clean and tidy.  Staff friendly ++++.  Other family stayed in cabin.  Plenty of room and again clean and tidy.  1km walk to beach.  Or drive.  We stayed for Hervey Bay Tri series.  Staff allowed us to stay longer after the Sunday morning race and pack up later in the day.   Thank you.

Heerlijk rustige camping met vriendelijk ontvangst. Direct aan het strand en supermarkt en restaurants op loopafstand. Veel verschillende vogels en om 6 uur vliegen de vleermuizen uit die overdag in een boom op de camping slapen. Super plek
Nice clean park central to everything.
Nice clean cabins

Friendly greeting. Nice clean park. Lots of permanent people who keep their places immaculate. Plenty of toilets and showers. Dump point near the amenities. Nice and clean too. Pretty quiet too except for a local's yappy little dog ar 4:30am!

We had a wonderful experience at Tasman Holiday Park. Despite the pouring rain, we had a big room that accommodated us comfortably. The reasonable price and its convenient location close to everywhere made our stay even more enjoyable. Highly recommended for a memorable holiday.

Great park has lots of options from cabins to campsites to suit all needs. I had a powered site which was well maintained and clean. The park itself is clean and staff very friendly as well as good price. Quiet as well and but close to everything you need.

Great park in a good location which was very dog friendly. A park right next to it for the dog to go for a run. Walking distance to the beachfront and shops. Friendly staff and amenities were excellent.

I cannot recommend this Tasman holiday park enough. We love getting the pool cue/balls (small deposit needed). The pool is always refreshing. The rooms are well equipped, spacious and the beds are comfortable. This park is now our favourite place to stay in Hervey Bay.

A Member of the G'Day Parks Group. Have attended this park with our caravan on a couple of occasions. Pets allowed to sites only with conditions. Well maintained park, with friendly ground staff, and office staff. An excellent camp Kitchen, 2 BBQ"s etc, large seating area.||Highly recommended for packpackers, vanners, or family wishing to use the many cabins.
Fantastic bungalow and wonderful staff. Our home away from home.
Just a few little things in the room that made me take a star off but nothing major. We have stayed in the past with excellent results.

Sadly a disgraceful out come for me and my quadraplegic friend yesterday after being recommend this park.On arrival the manager stated we would have to pay for 2 sites as we have a car towing a trailer .My friend has his car modified for him to sleep in as it has rails etc for him to adjust sleeping positions.I as this Carer sleep in a small rooftop tent ontop of a small trailer so therefore we have 2 dwellings.The manager was very adamant about this and therefore their was no point arguing about it.We certainly were not prepared to pay for 2 sites.We travel extensively around this beautiful country and this is the first time we have encountered anything like this.Hervey Bay council should be ashamed of themselves as should the Manager about this situation and the outcome.We sadly decided after this we would not stay in Burrum Heads and left the Shire altogether
The amenities are great!  It is spotless clean and the shower room is spacious. Sites are well designed and grounds are well kept.

From arrival the staff were very friendly and helpful. They assisted with directions  and then parking on site.|The park has recently undergone a full make-over and the hosts have done a fantastic job of maintaining the  grounds and amenities. Some of the best facilities we have found at a caravan park.|Big shout out to Kym and Sean and their friendly team.

Recently renovated park and facilities that are some of the best we have encountered.  Shout out to Kym & Sean (the Park Managers) and their staff. Very friendly, helpful and do a fantastic job of maintaining the grounds and amenities.
Highly recommend the Fraser Coast Beachfront Tourist Park at Burrum Heads, Queensland.

Although this was one of the pricier caravan parks we have stayed in, the phrase ' you get what you pay for ' is appropriate in this case. The ammenities were immaculate and one of the best designs we have seen. The grounds were beautifully maintained . We stayed in the Southern Pet friendly part of the park on a spacious site for our 22 ft caravan. I witnessed the exchange of words 2 days ago and my recollection is  very different.  The most raised voice did not come from the Managers. Our interactions with the Managers has been nothing but cheerful and most welcoming, and that doesn't change from one stay to another. Your comment about a 10am checkout time puzzles me because we find that time to be the norm for most parks. Unfortunately their will never be a time for amenities to be cleaned where it doesn't cause inconvenience, but I bet we'd complain long and hard if they were cleaned hastily or not at all. Again the comment about being closed for hours puzzles me, the sign says closed 8.30am to 9.30am, in our last 11 days since entering the park, we've found an average time of around 9.10am or 9.15am and the amenities are open again. Of course when the beachfront side opens, the problem of amenities not being available at all times won't exist. So close now to this being the case. We were made very aware of the cleaning times upon checkin, can only imagine that all guests are given the same information. As for the relaxing of vans needing to be offsite by 10am, the Managers would need every bit of time they can get to have the sites ready by 12noon for the arrival of new guests. This park has a midday checkin time, unlike a lot of parks these days  checkin can be 2pm. We are fortunate.  Perhaps a stroll to the office asking for an extra half hour may have been appropriate. The Managers have yet to conquer the control of the weather, they'll have to work on that, but geez, it was highly publicised that the weekend was going to be filthy, you must have been aware as you would have driven in it. Emails unfortunately are the done thing, it is the way of the business world. Again when I wish to speak personally with management, they've always reacted to my quick email asking them to give me a buzz when convenient. Works for us.  Beautiful new park, perfect location, amazing amenities and accommodating Managers, pretty spot on in our eyes. By the way, your theatrical exit with spinning of tyres was enough to entice others from their vans to see what the heck was going on. Not a good look look you!! We hope you go on to find that perfect park on the water.

You would think they would be a little more chill considering we were packing up. Also they don’t take phone calls only emails, don’t see how that is running a caravan park. Can be the best location and best facility’s but bad management ruin a park. Heaps of other parks in the area right on the water go there.

Great spot host was very helpful

What a stunning place to stay. My favourite so far on the east coast. Friendly staff, great amenities and right on the beach! We parked up the van and went for a walk on the beach to find dolphins playing right in front of us! Magical. Would definitely recommend.
Clean, quiet nice view, great place to relax and kick back.
Very friendly & close to everything

Our 2nd visit to this caravan park and loved it both times. The managers Jess and Darren are fantastic and very friendly, gave us the best sea view pitch and they keep the ammenaties in immaculate condition. It is very central and we cycled every day (it is all nice and flat 😍). We will be back!
Perfectly clean and friendly staff
Awesome caravan park, new refurbished facilities and close to the beach. Definitely will stay there again.
Excellent caravan park, good facilities.
Awesome but must book to be sure of a table in the mornings

Very friendly and helpful staff. Nice, new clean facilities.  Concrete slab and full grass on sites.  Very easy access straight onto the beach.  Beautiful!
Good facilities, easy beach access, some road noise.
Had a wonderful 10 days here in early July 2025 , nicely laid out , great sites , we were beachfront , nice new clean amenities , helpful staff look forward to staying again

Welcoming and helpful, thank you!
It ticks most boxes & shoulnt disappoint
Very tidy and clean. Amazing views and managers very welcoming and accommodating. Walking distance to all places. Great spot

I really don't want to make this post as I don't want to encourage anyone else to know about this truly stunning spot. We try and not repeat trips, but this place keeps drawing us back.

Right on the waterfront. Food works store across the road. Other takeaway as well. Torquay pub just a  minutes walk away. Beautiful and quiet. Amenities clean and tidy.  Staff very helpful.  Couldn't rate this place any better. Well done guys and girls.
Great spot by the beach at Torquay. Never rains in Hervey bay ... Except always at Easter. Doesn't matter - even in a tent, it's worth it.
Older caravan park but facilities very clean staff lovely very helpful.
Yes your love

Lovely spot, good service. Could cool it with the amount of security cameras around the site, felt like I was on big brother. Bit of a stretch asking guests to mop the showers. Beautiful almost exclusive beach.

I have never been to a camp ground where the owners were this amazing. 2 out of 3 nights they came around with zooper doopers for the kids. Noticed that our ute wasn't at our site one night, so they followed up to make sure everything was secure and locked. I couldn't rate a place more highly.
Great place Jess and Darren great hosts

We have been coming here every year after Christmas for over 30 years. The caretakers Jess and Darren have been the best ever. Noisy van park from traffic on esplanade, bit run down,not worth the small site  for price.

Very noisy from esplanade traffic, needs bigger sites.

Frazer Coast Beach front Tourist Park. This campground has been fully renovated recently. I would say it is the gold standard in the Hervey Bay area. Beautiful location right on the beachfront. Lovely beach for safe swimming. Helpful staff. Great sunset spot. Location is good for day trips to K'gari. Would definitely come back here again and recommend to others.

A great little caravan park with beach front sites available.

Spent the long weekend here with the little kids. Had a blast, will definitly be coming back in the near future
Spent a few nights with the van , great little clean park , friendly assistive staff , super close to a sheltered beach ,

We always wanted to stay at this place for many years before we purchased a caravan as its the best location in town.  Our first trip north from brisbane we had to make a booking, We managed to jag a spot along the beach front. Stunning views and early morning walks along the beach.

Megan very kindly sorted me out with a whale watching tour, organising the courtesy bus to pick me up and bring me back at the bus stop right out the very front. Bins were also easy to access.
Nice van Park, clean facilities, roomy sites, easy beach access.

Location is very good. On thr beach yet close to shops restaurants. A shared pathway that runs the length of the foreshore runs right past.

Scarness Caravan Park has been a fantastic stay !! Darren has been so helpful for a solo traveller backing onto the site. He has happily answered all my questions about power hook up. The amenities are very clean and all utilities full:- paper towels, soaps, toilet paper etc. The camp kitchen ever so spotless clean. All electric appliances working. He is very friendly even after a long week and the ever late check-ins. My first caravan park stay in my new motor home was excellent!!

This is a well looked after park. Spotless and modern amenities. (could do with an extra hook or 2 in shower cubicle) Top camp kitchen.  Washers/dryers plentiful and $4 a pop....fantastic..on the beach and opposite mini mart and abundance of eateries.

Other reviews correctly mention the beachfront location, good facilities, clean amenities, and good size sites....

What they DON'T mention is the high security risk and the CONSTANT traffic noise at all hours!

This is also the first park in 3 years of travelling Australia that has immediately given me a written "Lock it up or lose it" note!
🤔
Unusual perhaps? - No!! .. not when you take into account that they DON'T have an entrance boom-gate (or anything similar!) and lots of passing pedestrian/bike/scooter traffic..
Other campers warned me not to leave my e-bikes out - irrespective of them being locked! ... and one older guest actually parked his mobility trike inside the secured toilet/shower block overnight!
😬

The traffic noise rarely lets up, and there seems to be a competition amongst the locals to see who has the loudest exhaust!!

So ... you've been warned!!

Yes, it's arguably the nicest caravan park on the Esplenade... but bring your earplugs and padlocks!

Returned here to this beautiful park . Friendliest managers we have come across on our trip . So Happy &  helpful.   Thanks Kay & Phil you are really two beautiful people,  this world could do with more like you . We will be back again . Oh yes ! Großartiger campground, gaaaanz liebe Manager!!! Amenities first class, sites groß und rein, direkt an der Esplanade... Besser geht es nicht!

Hervey Bay’s premier beachfront caravan park ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

This park has benefited from a major refurbishment and now boasts fabulous facilities along with an awesome location 🏖️

All sites are level with ample space between, new concrete single slabs, new lighting and utility posts

All amenities are new and generously outfitted while the location is just perfect 👌

North facing Scarness Beach on one side and a generous variety of Esplanade cafes, restaurants, hotel, grocery store and news agent, among others, across the street 👍

We found the staff to be great to book with and friendly & helpful during our stay ❤️
It would seem it’s a nice place. The couple running the park would have to be the least suited individuals I’ve had the displeasure of dealing with to be running such a park.

Amenities are the best I have seen for a long time. Big dining area, big kitchen, showers and toilets are spotless, with the exception of that one person, that can read a sign.
Beach is pretty much on ya door step and the sea breeze kept it rather cool.

Would highly recommend this place to anyone.
Wonderful staff, great facilities, close to restaurants and bars.
Beautiful park, lovely green grass & slabbed sites. Park is right on the water. Amenities are new and immaculate.
Very nice well cared for. Great position.

We have spent several nights here over 2 trips, can't speak highly enough of this park, it is well managed, the facilities are modern, clean and well maintained and the location is 5 star you won't get a better beach front site at the price. Lovely check in staff! Very clean facilities and they have security during the night. Would definitely recommend to anyone.
The managers Phil and Kay were very helpful and professional during our stay. The park is kept immaculate, the only downside is the shower layout design. Overall we loved the place!

Fantastic Stay at Scarness Caravan Park -
We recently stayed at Scarness Caravan Park and couldn’t be happier with our experience. The park managers, Phil and Kay, were incredibly friendly and helpful. Kay was especially kind, assisting us in backing into our site. Not only does she keep the park spotless, but she is also continuously busy with maintenance, ensuring everything is in perfect condition, while at the same time,  being happy to stop for a pleasant chat, which added a personal touch to our stay.

The park itself boasts a beautiful beach front position in the heart of Hervey Bay,  with direct access shops and to a wide range of places to eat. There are generously sized blocks, and modern unisex amenities that are a spotlessly clean. Additionally, the shared outdoor kitchen is modern and clean, equipped with everything needed for a great meal outdoors.

We thoroughly enjoyed our time at Scarness Caravan Park and highly recommend it to anyone looking for a well-maintained and friendly place to stay. Whether you're here for a night or a longer holiday, Phil and Kay will make sure you feel right at home!
Great caravan park

Lovely spic and span and newly renovated. If anything a little clinical. Right on the beach. Amenities new and in a style we haven’t encountered, all individual unisex rooms. No issues with theft, just put valuable stuff away, which is a common theme with reviews of the foreshore parks. The sites are a reasonable size for a large van  but can be a bit tight to get in to as some of the new trees have been badly positioned. Its located across the road from some shops which is handy. The beach front sites are nice but if you cant get one the others are fine. Beautiful well appointed camp kitchen.
Sehr sauber, direkt am Strand, normale Preise, wenn man bedenkt, dass dies hier ein Topreiseziel ist.

This place is completely new and clean!
The beach was amazing and we really enjoyed the park here!

On top of it we had an issue with our RV and the owners were so kind - they let us come back after our check out and have our RV fixed in the park free of charge 🙏🏻 so grateful!

We stayed here 3 nights while visiting Fraser Island. Excellent location, very friendly staff and the installations were very nice and clean as well.

Amazing view to the beach and if you like running, this place is just next to the Esplanade that has a very long trail across the Coast.

Impeccable site. Extremely well maintained grounds and clean, modern amenities. We'll definitely be coming back.

Great park. Quiet and on the beach. All newly renovated.
Amazing location, lovely and accomodating staff. Family friendly, beautiful new facilities. Came back to stay twice, would highly recommend!

We had an AWESOME TIME at Scarness Beachfront Tourist Park camping for 8 days. All the camper were well behaved. All up I would give the Park a 10 out of 10. I really could not fault them. I will be more than happy to recommend this park to everyone I know.
Thank you so much.

Very comfortable, clean and a well run caravan park.

Great all round. We’re regulars and the whole place is spotless all the time. Managers Are always friendly and helpful and keep a general eye on security in the park.  Yesterday’s sunset from beachfront!

Nice little cv park..

A Good and Convenient place. Only 50m to the beach.

We stayed here for 26 days. In that time we have never been treated so well. We changed rooms each time the rooms were clean. The pool is amazing. We just want to say a BIG Thankyou tothe team specially Nat you are amazing. We will be back. Keep up the great work.

DREADFUL, DONT GO THERE.  Nothing like their brochure & website.  Old, dirty, rundown.  No-one mans the office during business hours, you have to ring a phone number to get someone.  I could not believe the state of the amenities block & on the day we arrived the pool & spa were disgusting, other tourists and ourselves wouldn't get in.  Your sites are in amongst the annuals that live there so our view next to us was a view of rubbish under & behind their van (this was at the front of the park, not down the back).  As we found out (2 weeks after leaving) from the people staying opposite & across from us on exactly the same type of site, we paid $15 more per night than they did......REALLY.  I have made contact with G'Day headquarters about the state of this park.  There a much nicer parks in Hervey Bay to stay.  See for yourself, check out the photos below.

The cabin I stayed in was amazing. Had everything I needed, was very spacious and clean! The pool in the complex was very nice and new. Location is perfect. The WiFi actually works! Staff were very friendly and helpful. Would recommend!
Fantastic stay, caretakers couldn’t be more helpful, close to beach and shops, little tight for big rigs, amenities good, pool and spa awesome , rec room and camp kitchen great also
Old and shabby, noisy apartment
Really nice little park, pet friendly, nice amenities, good location. Definitely will go back to visit again
Facilities need repairs. Huge variations in prices. Single female charged $59 per night whereas couple in next site paid $25!
Great value and close to everything
